reason: HyprMod exposes or schema-backs many remaining cursor/input rows, but does not provide cursor-input-specific recovery, rollback, confirmation, or dead-man proof. Since the remaining rows are visibility, hardware cursor, warping, zoom, or monitor-targeting critical, HyprMod evidence does not make any row safe enough for a future subset under the current recovery-independence rule.
